data.title: Palo Alto Networks EVP Sells Over $23 Million in Company Stock
data.description:
Reporter NameKlarich Lee
RelationshipEVP Chief Product & Tech Ofcr
TypeSell
Amount$23,081,150
SEC FilingForm 4

Palo Alto Networks' EVP Chief Product & Tech Officer, Klarich Lee, sold 120,774 shares of common stock on September 3, 2025, for a total of $23,081,150. The sales were executed at weighted average prices ranging from $188.604 to $192.223 per share, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on September 27, 2024. Following these transactions, Lee directly owns 177,954 shares and indirectly owns 740,000 shares through the Klarich 2005 Trust.

SEC Filing: Palo Alto Networks Inc [ PANW ] - Form 4 - Sep. 05, 2025

Several top executives at Automatic Data Processing (ADP) have recently sold shares of the company's stock, according to SEC Form 4 filings.

Carlos A Rodriguez, a Director at ADP, sold 23,402 shares across transactions on September 3 and 4, 2025, with prices per share ranging from $296.3607 to $301.292, totaling $7,004,406. Following these sales, Rodriguez directly owns 37,417 shares and indirectly owns 13,813 shares through a trust.

Don McGuire, Corp VP, sold 13,599 shares on September 3, 2025, at a price of $296.57 per share, totaling $4,033,055. McGuire now directly owns 17,684 shares.

David Foskett, another Corp. VP, sold 1,373 shares on September 3, 2025, at a price of $296.57 per share, totaling $407,190. Post-transaction, Foskett directly owns 11,780 shares.

Joseph DeSilva, Executive VP, sold 4,614 shares on September 3, 2025, at a price of $296.57 per share, totaling $1,368,373. DeSilva directly owns 17,535 shares following the sale.

Black Maria, President & CEO, sold 23,605 shares on September 3, 2025, with prices ranging from $295.9144 to $299.9789, resulting in a total sale amount of $7,041,744. Maria directly owns 76,161 shares after the transactions.

All sales were executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted in September 2024.

data.title: OOMA INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Ooma Inc., a leading provider of innovative communication solutions and services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter ended July 31, 2025. The report highlights the company's continued growth in both financial and operational metrics, driven primarily by the robust performance of Ooma Business. This summary provides an overview of the key financial and business highlights from the report.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $66.4 million, up 3% year-over-year, primarily driven by the growth of Ooma Business.
  • Gross Profit: $40.2 million, reflecting a gross margin of 61%, up from 60% in the prior year quarter.
  • Income (loss) from operations: $0.9 million, compared to a loss of $1.6 million in the prior year quarter, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $1.3 million, compared to a net loss of $2.1 million in the prior year quarter, reflecting continued improvement in operations.
  • Basic Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $0.05, compared to $(0.08) in the prior year quarter.
  • Diluted Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $0.04, compared to $(0.08) in the prior year quarter.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Ooma derived approximately 63% of its total revenue from Ooma Business and approximately 35% from Ooma Residential for the three months ended July 31, 2025. This indicates a strong performance in the business segment, driven by user growth and increased average revenue per core user.
  • Sales Units: The increase in product and other revenue by 15% year-over-year was primarily due to an increase in AirDial shipments, reflecting a positive trend in the sales of on-premise devices.
  • Operational Efficiency: Subscription and services gross margin remained consistent at 70% year-over-year, indicating stable operational efficiency in delivering services.
  • Product Margin Improvement: Product and other revenue gross margin improved significantly to negative 47% from negative 70% in the prior year period, primarily due to the depletion of higher cost components procured in prior fiscal years.
  • Research and Development: R&D expenses decreased by 8% year-over-year, primarily due to a reduction in personnel and contractor-related costs, reflecting a focus on cost management while maintaining innovation.
  • Future Outlook: Ooma expects its subscription and services revenue to grow as it expands its core user base, driven primarily by growth in Ooma Business. The company also anticipates an increase in sales and marketing expenses in absolute dollars as it continues to grow its business.

SEC Filing: OOMA INC [ OOMA ] - 10-Q - Sep. 05, 2025

data.title: DILLARD'S, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Dillard's, Inc., a prominent American department store chain, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, reflecting a stable yet challenging retail environment. Below are the key highlights from the report.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: Dillard's reported net sales of $1,513.8 million, marking an increase of $23.9 million or approximately 1.6% compared to the same period last year. This growth was driven by a 1% increase in retail operations and a 5.5% increase in the construction segment.

Gross Margin: The company's gross margin stood at $554.5 million, a decrease of $5.1 million or 0.9% from the previous year. The gross margin percentage was 36.6%, down from 37.6% last year, primarily due to a decrease in retail operations gross margin.

Income Before Income Taxes: Dillard's reported income before income taxes of $94.6 million, a slight decrease from $97.1 million in the previous year, indicating a stable operating environment despite a challenging retail landscape.

Net Income: The company's net income was $72.8 million, down from $74.5 million in the previous year. The net income margin was 4.8%, compared to 5.0% last year.

Earnings Per Share: Earnings per share (EPS) increased slightly to $4.66 from $4.59 in the previous year, reflecting a stable earnings performance despite a slight decrease in net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The retail operations segment experienced a 1% increase in net sales. Notable growth was seen in juniors’ and children’s apparel, which increased significantly. Ladies’ accessories and lingerie, and ladies’ apparel saw moderate increases, while shoes and men’s apparel and accessories experienced slight increases. However, cosmetics sales decreased slightly, and home and furniture sales decreased moderately.

Sales Units: The number of sales transactions decreased by 2% for both the three and six months ended August 2, 2025, compared to the same periods in 2024. However, the average dollars per sales transaction increased by 3% and 2%, respectively.

Future Outlook: Management is focused on inventory control in a rapidly changing operating environment. The company expects the fiscal 2025 federal and state effective income tax rate to approximate 23%. Dillard's is closely monitoring inflation and potential trade restrictions, including tariffs, which pose a risk to operations. The extent of the impact on financial performance will depend on the effectiveness of ongoing initiatives to manage these fluctuating costs.

SEC Filing: DILLARD'S, INC. [ DDS ] - 10-Q - Sep. 05, 2025

data.title: US judge blocks Trump from canceling legal status for Venezuelans, Haitians
data.description:
A federal judge in San Francisco ruled on Friday that President Donald Trump's administration violated federal law by moving to cancel temporary protections from deportation for more than 1 million Venezuelans and Haitians living in the United States.

U.S. District Judge Edward Chen said that Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem lacked the authority to end the program, known as Temporary Protected Status or TPS, and the decision to do so was motivated by racism against Venezuelan and Haitian people.

"There is no evidence of any reasoned decision making" behind Secretary Noem's finding that the program should be canceled, Chen wrote.

Trump, a Republican, has made a crackdown on legal and illegal immigration a central plank of his second White House term. Canceling TPS protections would be a major boost to the administration's campaign to deport millions of people.

Chen in March had temporarily blocked the cancellation of TPS status granted to about 600,000 Venezuelans during the administration of Trump's Democratic predecessor, President Joe Biden. Haitian TPS recipients later joined the case.

The March ruling was upheld by an appeals court last week, but has been paused by the U.S. Supreme Court.

The administration is likely to appeal Friday's decision. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Temporary Protected Status is available to people whose home country has experienced a natural disaster, armed conflict or other extraordinary event. It provides eligible migrants with work authorization and temporary protection from deportation.

The program was created in 1991 and under Biden was extended to cover about 600,000 Venezuelans and 521,000 Haitians. Noem reversed the extensions in February, saying they were no longer justified.

But Chen on Friday agreed with a group representing TPS recipients that Noem made that decision without first consulting with relevant federal agencies or conducting a comprehensive review of conditions in the two countries.

data.title: TWIN DISC INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Twin Disc Inc., a leading manufacturer of power transmission equipment, has released its Form 10-K report for fiscal 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment. Twin Disc's diverse product portfolio includes marine transmissions, azimuth drives, surface drives, propellers, boat management systems, power-shift transmissions, hydraulic torque converters, power take-offs, industrial clutches, control systems, and braking systems. The company operates globally with manufacturing facilities in the United States, Europe, and Asia, and distribution locations worldwide.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $340.7 million, reflecting a 15.5% increase from fiscal 2024, driven by acquisitions and strong growth in the Veth product.
  • Gross Profit: $92.7 million, an increase of 11.3% from the previous year, with a gross profit margin of 27.2%, impacted by improved volumes and a less favorable product mix.
  • Income from Operations: $9.9 million, a decrease from the prior year, influenced by increased marketing, engineering, and administrative expenses.
  • Net (Loss) Income: $(1.6) million, compared to a net income of $11.2 million in fiscal 2024, affected by higher interest expenses and other income changes.
  • Basic (Loss) Income Per Share: $(0.14), reflecting the net loss attributable to Twin Disc, Incorporated common shareholders.

Business Highlights

  • Product Lines: Twin Disc designs, manufactures, and sells a variety of power transmission equipment, which have consistently accounted for more than 90% of the company's revenues over the past three fiscal years.
  • Geographical Performance: Sales to the U.S. and Canada improved by 10% in fiscal 2025, while sales in the Asia Pacific market decreased by 20% due to softening demand in China. European market sales improved by approximately 40%, driven by strong demand for Veth propulsion products and the addition of Katsa revenue.
  • Sales Units: The company's marine transmission and propulsion systems saw a 17.1% increase in fiscal 2025, reflecting strong market conditions and easing supply chain constraints. The off-highway transmission market experienced a 2.1% increase, primarily due to high demand for ARFF transmissions. Industrial products saw a 61.8% increase, driven by demand in North American construction and recycling markets.
  • New Acquisitions: In fiscal 2025, Twin Disc acquired Kobelt Manufacturing Co. Ltd., a Canadian manufacturer of controls, propulsion, steering, and braking systems, and Katsa Oy, a Finnish manufacturer of power transmission components and gearboxes. These acquisitions are expected to enhance and diversify the company's product portfolio.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced challenges in the European industrial and commercial marine markets, with a 29.7% decrease in sales from its Italian operations due to the sale of the BCS business. The Belgian operations saw an 18.4% decrease in sales due to softer demand in European marine markets.
  • Future Outlook: Management expects to drive inventory reductions through fiscal 2026 and anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $17 million to $19 million to invest in modern equipment for growth, efficiencies, and quality improvements. The company remains focused on leveraging its global sales and service teams to drive success following recent acquisitions.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Acquisitions: The company completed the acquisition of Kobelt Manufacturing Co., Ltd. and Katsa Oy to enhance its product portfolio and expand its global presence. These acquisitions are part of the company's strategy to diversify its offerings and strengthen its position in the power transmission solutions market.
  • Credit Agreement: Twin Disc entered into an amended and restated Credit Agreement with Bank of Montreal to refinance and replace its prior credit agreement, which supports its strategic acquisitions and capital needs.
  • Capital Management: The company did not repurchase any shares during fiscal 2025, leaving 315,000 shares authorized for repurchase under its existing plan. It paid dividends totaling $2.6 million and increased its borrowings to finance acquisitions. The company maintained a strong balance sheet with a current ratio of 2.0 and had approximately $32.1 million of available borrowings under the Credit Agreement as of June 30, 2025. The company also managed its capital expenditures, investing $15.2 million in property, plant, and equipment to support growth and operational efficiency.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue leveraging its Credit Agreement to support strategic acquisitions and capital investments. It expects to drive inventory reductions through fiscal 2026 and anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $17 million to $19 million in fiscal 2026. The company remains focused on maintaining sufficient liquidity and capital resources to fund its operations and strategic initiatives, with an emphasis on sustainable growth and operational improvements.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Competition: Twin Disc faces significant competition in its markets, including from divisions of larger manufacturers and parts manufacturing divisions of major customers. The primary competitive factors are design, technology, performance, price, service, and availability.
  • Customer Concentration: The company's top ten customers accounted for a significant portion of sales, indicating a reliance on key customers.
  • Currency Fluctuations: The company is exposed to currency fluctuations, particularly between the U.S. dollar and the euro, which could adversely affect profitability.
  • Health Pandemics: Health pandemics or epidemics pose risks such as diminished demand, supply chain interruptions, and increased costs.
  • Market Cyclicality: The cyclical nature of the oil and gas market and other product markets could materially affect financial performance.
  • Raw Material Shortages: Increasing global demand for steel could lead to shortages of raw materials, impacting production schedules and profitability.
  • Commodity Costs and Trade Policies: Rising commodity costs and trade policy uncertainties, including tariffs, could adversely affect profitability.
  • Key Customer and Supplier Risks: The loss of key customers or suppliers could disrupt business operations.
  • International Risks: International sales and operations expose the company to risks such as political and economic instability, currency fluctuations, and differing legal standards.
  • Operational Disruptions: A significant disruption at a major manufacturing facility could impact sales and customer demand.
  • Debt Servicing: The ability to service debt depends on generating cash flow and refinancing, with potential adverse effects if financial covenants are not met.
  • PPP Loan Audit: The company remains subject to audit for its PPP loan, with potential repayment and reputational risks.
  • Acquisition Risks: Assumptions related to acquisitions may prove inaccurate, affecting integration and expected benefits.
  • Intangible Assets: The company carries significant intangible assets, which may be impaired if certain conditions arise.
  • Tax Challenges: Tax challenges and changes in tax laws could lead to unexpected tax payments.
  • Security Breaches: Security breaches could compromise information systems, leading to liabilities and reputational damage.

SEC Filing: TWIN DISC INC [ TWIN ] - 10-K - Sep. 05, 2025

data.title: ICE canola futures fall as market digests China news, government supports
data.description:
ICE canola futures fell on Friday as the market digested Chinese government action and Canadian government supports for its industry.

• November canola (RSX5) settled down $3.40 at $616.80 per metric ton. January (RSF6) settle down $3.90 at $628.20.

• China announced its preliminary tariffson Canadian canola seed will be extended until March, a move some see as a way of allowing trade negotiations to have more time.

• The Canadian government announced support for Canadian biofuels production on Friday, including subsidies for domestic production and a pledge to fix regulations that the canola industry has long called broken.

• Chicago Board of Trade soyoil futures (BOv1) fell 1.35%.

• Euronext rapeseed futures (COMc1) were flat.

• Malaysian palm oil futures rose 0.07%.

• The Canadian dollar fell for a fifth straight session.

data.title: US yields dive as markets bet big on Fed cuts after dismal jobs print
data.description:
Key points:
  • US economy creates just 22,000 jobs, yields tumble
  • US two-year, 10-year yields fall to five-month low
  • US 2/10 yield curve initially steepens after jobs data
  • US rate futures start to price in small chance of 50-bp cut

U.S. Treasury yields slumped on Friday after data showed the world's largest economy created far fewer jobs than expected in August, reinforcing expectations the Federal Reserve will resume cutting interest rates at a policy meeting this month.

The rate futures market has also started to price in a small probability of a 50 basis-point easing at the end of the two-day meeting on September 17.

In afternoon trading, U.S. two-year yields, which reflect interest rate expectations, and the benchmark 10-year yield both fell to their lowest since April.

The two-year yield was last down 7.9 basis points (bps) at 3.513% (US2YT=RR). It was down 11 bps on the week, the biggest weekly fall since late July.

The benchmark 10-year yield sagged 8.8 bps to 4.088% . On the week, the yield fell nearly 14 bps, the largest weekly decline since late July as well.

Data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics showed that U.S. nonfarm payrolls increased by only 22,000 jobs last month after rising by an upwardly revised 79,000 in July, the Labor Department said on Friday.

Economists polled by Reuters had forecast payrolls rising by 75,000 positions after a previously reported 73,000 gain in July.

Data showed that in June, the U.S. economy lost 13,000 jobs instead of the initially reported 14,000 payrolls gain.

"The warning bell that rang in the labor market a month ago just got louder. A weaker-than-expected jobs report all but seals a 25-basis-point rate cut later this month," said Olu Sonola, head of U.S. economic research at Fitch Ratings in New York.

"The Fed is likely to prioritize labor market stability over its inflation mandate, even as inflation drifts further from the 2% target. Four straight months of manufacturing job losses stand out."

Following the data, the U.S. rate futures market has priced in a 7% chance that the Fed will cut by 50 bps later this month, and a 93% probability of the more standard 25 bp cut, according to LSEG calculations. The 50-bps bet was as high as 15% earlier in the session.

Traders have also priced in about 71 bps of easing this year, or about three cuts of 25-bps rate declines per meeting. That was up from 59 bps on Thursday.

The Treasury yield curve, meanwhile, initially steepened following the jobs report, with the gap between two-year and 10-year yields widening to 59.6 bps (US2US10=TWEB), compared with 57.1 bps late on Thursday. On Wednesday, the curve hit 63.8 bps, its widest spread since April.

The steeper curve suggested that traders are pricing an imminent rate cut from the Fed. But it also reflected inflation worries if the Fed cuts too aggressively, which have prompted investors to sell the long end of the curve.

The yield curve was last at 56.2 bps.

In other Treasury maturities, the yield on U.S. 30-year bonds, the focus of an investor sell-off early this week, fell to a two-month low and was last down 10.2 bps at 4.7% 71 (US30YT=RR). The yield dropped 15 bps this week, the largest weekly decline since late March.

On Wednesday, 30-year yields touched 5% for the first time, since July.

For next week, the key data will be the U.S. consumer prices index (CPI) for August. A Reuters poll showed headline CPI of 0.3% last month and a year-on-year rate of 2.9%.

"A softer-than-expected core inflation print (0.1% below the month-on-month consensus) increases the likelihood that the Fed cuts in October and subsequent meetings," wrote Wells Fargo in a research note.

We think the risks are relatively symmetric around next week’s CPI report, but the composition will matter."

data.title: The Jobs Report Was Bad. It's Hitting Bank and Brokerage Stocks. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Andrew Welsch

Shares of Charles Schwab, Robinhood, other brokerage firms, and banks dropped after a disappointing jobs report raised concerns the economy is weakening and increased the likelihood the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates.

On Friday, the Bureau of Labor Statistics said the U.S. economy added just 22,000 jobs in August, well below the 76,500 analysts were expecting. The government also revised nonfarm payrolls for June and July lower. If the Federal Reserve lowers interest rates to spur economic growth, that likely would hurt net interest income, a key source of profit for banks, brokerages, and wealth management companies. NII is the difference between what banks earn on interest-bearing assets and the interest they pay to depositors.

"Softer economic data this morning, broader equity market pressure, and the prospect of falling interest rates are all inputs that could impact brokerage stocks. Interest-rate implications for brokerage firms are nuanced, with puts and takes, but lower rates could create some headwinds," said Devin Ryan, equity research analyst at Citizens. Financial stocks were dropping Friday. The Vanguard Financials ETF, which tracks a basket of financial companies, was down 1.7%, versus a 0.5% decline for the S&P 500 index. The KBW Bank Index was down 1.8%. Schwab's stock was down 5.5%. Robinhood and Interactive Brokers shares were down 3.9% and 5.9%, respectively. Morgan Stanley and Stifel Financial, both which have wealth management and investment bank units, dropped 1.7% and 2.5%, respectively.

The nation's largest consumer banks also took hits: Wells Fargo's stock declined 3.0%. Shares of Bank of America and JPMorgan Chase fell 1.7% and 2.6%, respectively.

Friday's jobs report adds to recent economic data pointing to a jobs market and economy under pressure from tariffs and trade policy uncertainty. Sluggish economic growth, or even a recession, could hurt banks by dampening demand for loans and spurring more defaults. Brokerage and wealth management clients may also be less inclined to put more money in their investing accounts. Shareholders have been keeping close tabs on the ability of Schwab and other companies to bring in net new assets.

Investors' Friday jitters contrast with recent bullish sentiment and strong second-quarter earnings at banks and brokerage firms, which were lifted by a trading boom as brokerage customers borrowed from their firms to buy stocks. Margin debt topped $1 trillion in June, a record, according to data from Finra, the brokerage industry's self-regulatory organization. Shares of Schwab and Interactive Brokers are up this year 24% and 37%, respectively. Robinhood's stock is up 157% even after a sharp selloff in stocks in April when President Donald Trump unveiled sweeping tariffs.

Whether investors will ultimately brush off today's bad news as a temporary setback remains to be seen.

Write to Andrew Welsch at andrew.welsch@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Apparel retailer Children's Place Q2 sales fall 6.8%
data.description:
Overview

  • Children's Place fiscal Q2 net sales fall 6.8% to $298 mln

  • Adjusted net loss was $3.4 mln, compared to adjusted net income of $3.9 mln last year

  • Co announces transformation initiative to yield $40 mln in benefits over three years

Outlook

  • Company projects $20 mln to $25 mln in additional tariff expenses for FY 2025

  • Children's Place plans to mitigate 80% of tariff impacts through strategic initiatives

  • Company expects $40 mln in gross benefits from streamlining operations over three years

  • Children's Place to launch new loyalty program in Q3 to enhance customer retention

Result Drivers

  • BACK-TO-SCHOOL MOMENTUM - Co reports strong sales momentum during back-to-school season, improving comparable sales

  • LICENSING AND PARTNERSHIPS - Expansion of licensing and new partnerships resonated with core customers, enhancing brand value

  • INVENTORY MANAGEMENT - Co reduced inventory by $78 mln from prior year, focusing on working capital management

data.description:
** S&P 500 finishes up 0.3% as economic slowdown worries cloud rate-cut optimism

** Dow loses 0.3%, yet the Nasdaq Composite adds 1.1%

** U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield sags to around 4.09%, lowest since Apr, for 3rd consecutive week of declines

** In the end, just a minority of sectors shine: Communication Services and Consumer Discretionary most bright, while Financials and Energy in a fog

** Communication Services rally 5.1%. Alphabet vaults to record high as Google empire dodges antitrust breakup demand, and tech sector component Apple rises on the news

** Consumer Discretionary gains 1.6%. Tesla accelerates on record $1 trillion performance-linked pay package for CEO Musk

U.S. homebuilders including D.R. Horton , Lennar and Pultegroup climb as yields tumble after weak jobs report

In contrast, Lululemon sinks 17% on the week as profit outlook tightens again, hurt by weak U.S. business and tariff costs

** Consumer Staples up 0.3%. However, Kraft Heinz drops after announcing split into two companies, dismantling the merger which was backed by Berkshire Hathaway and 3G Capital

And Corona beer maker Constellation Brands spills after slashing annual forecast due to soft demand

On Fri, Kenvue plunges after report RFK Jr. plans to link Tylenol use during pregnancy to autism

** Tech ticks up 0.2%. Broadcom jumps after disclosing $10 bln AI chip deal, reportedly with OpenAI. AVGO notches 13% weekly gain

Semiconductor index up 1.6%

But Salesforce slides after weak qtrly rev forecast signals delayed AI returns

** Financials fade 1.7%. Big banks fall on Fri as labor market cracks widen

For the week, S&P 500 banks index (.SPXBK) down 2%, KBW regional banking index up fractionally

** Energy sheds 3.5%. Group drops as crude prices decline on higher supply concerns, surprise increase in inventories

Arch Capital Group Boosts Buyback Authorization by $2 Billion
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Arch Capital Group increased its stock buyback authorization by $2 billion.

The insurance and reinsurance company said Friday that counting the higher authorization and third-quarter buybacks about $2.3 billion of stock remained available for repurchase as of Thursday.

Arch repurchased about $163 million of stock in the second quarter

Shares were up 1% to $92.20 in after-hours trading.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: SIGNET JEWELERS LTD SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Signet Jewelers Ltd., a leading retailer of diamond jewelry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the 13 weeks ended August 2, 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency, reflecting the company's strategic initiatives and market positioning.

Financial Highlights

Total Sales: Signet Jewelers reported total sales of $1,535.1 million for the 13 weeks ended August 2, 2025, an increase from $1,491.0 million for the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by an uptick in both merchandise and service sales.

Gross Margin: The company's gross margin rose to $591.9 million, up from $566.3 million in the prior year, indicating improved cost management and pricing strategies.

Operating Income: Signet achieved an operating income of $2.8 million, a significant turnaround from an operating loss of $100.9 million in the same period in 2024, highlighting enhanced operational efficiency.

Net (Loss) Income: The net loss for the period was $(9.1) million, a substantial improvement from the net loss of $(98.5) million in the previous year.

Earnings (Loss) Per Common Share - Basic: The basic earnings per share improved to $(0.22) from $(2.28) in the same period in 2024.

Earnings (Loss) Per Common Share - Diluted: Similarly, the diluted earnings per share were $(0.22), consistent with the basic EPS, indicating no dilution effect.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The North America segment reported sales of $2,877.2 million for the 26 weeks ended August 2, 2025, with significant contributions from brands like Kay, Zales, and Jared. The International segment contributed $171.9 million, while the Other segment, which includes diamond sourcing operations, added $27.6 million.

Geographical Performance: The North America segment, encompassing operations in the US and Canada, remains the largest contributor to sales, with a notable presence in both online and brick-and-mortar retail operations. The International segment, operating in the UK and Republic of Ireland, showed stable performance with sales of $171.9 million.

Sales Units: As of August 2, 2025, Signet operated 2,623 retail locations, with 2,273 locations in the US and 91 in Canada, highlighting its extensive retail footprint.

Future Outlook: The company is focused on its Grow Brand Love strategy, which includes optimizing its store fleet and enhancing brand structure. This strategy aims to improve profitability and align margins with the rest of the business over the next few years.

SEC Filing: SIGNET JEWELERS LTD [ SIG ] - 10-Q - Sep. 05, 2025
